controls.label: Change amount
controls.hint: Adjust how much the video changes from the original. Higher values create more dramatic changes, while lower values keep it closer to the source.

controls.label: Creativity strength
controls.hint: Adjust how much freedom the AI has to create. Higher values generate more creative but less predictable results, while lower values stay closer to your original prompt.

controls.label: Speed vs. Quality
controls.hint: Adjust the balance between generation speed and output quality. Higher values produce better quality but take longer. Upscaler highly encouraged for best quality.

controls.label: Subject
controls.hint: Choose the subject type of your video for best results. This affects how the AI analyzes your video.

controls.label: Creativity
controls.hint: Lower values stick closer to your reference, while higher values reimagine the scene more freely.

controls.label: Control Guidance
controls.hint: On: guide generation with extracted control signals below. Off: your raw video guides generation directly.

controls.label: Pose Control
controls.hint: Guide generation using the pose structure of your reference.

controls.label: Depth Control
controls.hint: Guide generation using the depth map of your reference.

controls.label: Edge Control
controls.hint: Guide generation using the edge outlines of your reference.

controls.label: Use Anchor
controls.hint: Enable an anchor image that sets the starting frame and identity anchor for the generated video.

controls.label: Anchor Image
controls.hint: Upload an image to set the starting frame and identity anchor for the generated video.

controls.label: End Frame
controls.hint: Enable an end frame image that the generated video should reach by its final frame.

controls.label: End Frame Image
controls.hint: Upload an image the generated video should arrive at by its final frame.

controls.label: End Frame Strength
controls.hint: How strongly the end frame image pulls the final frames toward it. 1.0 locks the last frame exactly.

controls.label: Speed Priority
controls.hint: Higher speed may reduce quality but will make generation time faster. It is recommended to run previews at a high speed to get the gist of the result, and full generations at full quality.

controls.label: Style Transfer
controls.hint: Upload an image to transfer its style. Adjust strength to control the intensity.

controls.label: Full Body Replacement
controls.hint: Replaces the subject of the video with a character of your choosing. The subject is detected automatically. WARNING: increases processing time significantly.

controls.label: Body Replacement Image
controls.hint: Upload a full-body image of the character you want to use for replacement.

controls.label: Face Swap
controls.hint: Upload a face image to replace all faces in the video with your uploaded face. Adjust strength as needed.

controls.label: Face Expression Transfer
controls.hint: Transfers facial expressions from the original video to the new video.

controls.label: Effects
controls.hint: Choose a card to apply special effects. Adjust strength to control the intensity.

controls.label: Detailer
controls.hint: Tells the generation to add more details to the face, the hands, or the person in your creation. May increase generation time.

controls.label: Skin Improvement
controls.hint: Slightly improves the quality of skin tones and textures in the output.

controls.label: Increase output resolution
controls.hint: Increase your video's detail, sharpness, and resolution.

controls.label: Upscale multiplier
controls.hint: Choose how much to increase the resolution by.

controls.label: Seed Control
controls.hint: Control the randomness of generation. Random creates unique results each time, while Static allows reproducible results.

controls.label: Seed
controls.hint: The seed used when Seed Control is set to Static (-1 = random).

controls.label: 'Creativity' Adjustments
controls.hint: In AI, temperature refers to how "creative" you want the model to be. I added a few configurations that sometimes work and sometimes don't. Enabling model temperature adjustments may produce unexpected results and encourages your experimentation.

controls.label: Sampler
controls.hint: Choose the sampling method for your video generation. Recommended (multistep/dpmpp_2m): Best balance of quality and speed. See the full list of options in the dropdown for detailed descriptions. Only change this setting if you know what you're doing.

controls.label: Scheduler
controls.hint: Choose the scheduler method for noise reduction. Recommended (sgm_uniform): Best balance for most videos. Simple: A basic, fast scheduler. Karras: Enhanced detail preservation. Exponential: Smooth, progressive noise reduction. DDIM Uniform: A variation of the classic DDIM scheduler. Beta: A versatile scheduler that adapts well to different content types. Normal: A standard noise reduction schedule. Linear Quadratic: A quadratic-based noise reduction schedule. KL Optimal: Optimized for Kullback-Leibler divergence. Bong Tangent: An experimental scheduler with a unique noise reduction curve. Beta57: An experimental scheduler with a specific beta distribution. Only change this setting if you know what you're doing.

controls.label: Scheduler
controls.hint: Choose the scheduler method for Forge Inspire mode. Recommended (DPMSolverMultistepScheduler): Best balance for inspire mode. Creative (FlowMatchDiscreteScheduler): Emphasizes creative transitions. Enhanced (SDE-DPMSolverMultistepScheduler): More detailed generation. Adaptive (SASolverScheduler): Adjusts to video content dynamically. Precise (UniPCMultistepScheduler): Focuses on accuracy in generation. Only change this setting if you know what you're doing.
